metrics.narrative: Pain is well articulated but not yet financially quantified. Sophie Onyeks described the council's scale and manual burden on the 22 Apr 2026 discovery call ('our income per annum is now 1,700,000,000... we've basically got to go with best practice... get rid of all these manual working processes'), and the CRM MEDDPICC record (updated 7 Jul 2026) frames the goal as Procurement Act 2023 compliance and governance/transparency improvement across roughly 65 procurement officers and circa 3,000 active contracts. No $ savings target or ROI figure has been stated by either side to date.

champion.narrative: No confirmed champion. The CRM MEDDPICC record (7 Jul 2026) itself rates the council's RFI-coordinating contact (Interim Head of IT Category Management, Digital & Technology Services) as only a 'tentative' champion with status 'not yet established.' Sophie Onyeks is warm and responsive across every email thread and the 22 Apr 2026 call, but explicitly disclaimed evaluator influence on that call: 'I'm not gonna be one of the big people doing the evaluations... I'm literally just running things... I'm a grunt worker.' No one with confirmed access to John Coyne or Lucy Moody has yet advocated for Zycus.

competition.narrative: The council is running an 8-way competitive RFI per the CRM MEDDPICC record (updated 7 Jul 2026), corroborated on the 22 Apr 2026 call by Sophie Onyeks: 'we've reached out to 7 suppliers.' The only rival independently named in Zycus's own records is the incumbent, Intent, whose contract expires in August 2027 per the current Next_Step__c ('working back from the August 2027 incumbent Intent contract expiry'). The identities of the other rivals are not yet confirmed by a buyer quote and Competitors__c is blank in Salesforce.

identify_pain.narrative: Rich, buyer-voiced pain from the 22 Apr 2026 call: 'we've got two systems at the moment... neither of them does what we need to do in terms of contract management... the team are at the moment using a lot of Excel spreadsheets... using SharePoint for documents'; 'there is no standard way of procurement coming into the procurement team at the moment... there isn't even a sort of Excel sheet that you fill out.' Sophie Onyeks could not produce a supplier-risk report on demand ('that would be too difficult') and confirmed spend/contract data lives only manually or in Oracle ERP for authorised spend.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics discussed yet; the deal is pre-shortlist. The one live paper-adjacent risk is procurement-route compliance: the RFI states the council's preferred route is the RM6285 Lot 2 framework, and on the 22 Apr 2026 call Zycus's own VP EMEA disclosed 'we're not in any g cloud frameworks currently... we've just applied for Crown Commercial Services, so that will be on there... it said June is when we'll be on there' -- with framework status still unresolved on the current Next Step. Current_Contract_Expiration__c is blank in Salesforce even though the incumbent Intent's contract expiry (Aug 2027) is named directly in Next_Step__c -- a hygiene gap, not a knowledge gap.

economic_buyer.narrative: John Coyne (Procurement Director) carries the Decision Maker contact role in Salesforce, and Sophie Onyeks named him on the 22 Apr 2026 call as the leadership-level sponsor ('our director, the procurement director... John Coyne'). Lucy Moody (Assistant Director, Strategic Procurement Operations) is titled Executive Sponsor and was confirmed as the programme sponsor by Sophie on that same call ('the sponsor is a lady called Lucy Moody... she's assistant director level... she reports into John'). Neither John Coyne nor Lucy Moody has had any direct contact with Zycus since that single discovery call; the CRM MEDDPICC record (7 Jul 2026) still states budget ownership is 'not stated in the RFI - to be confirmed.' Identification exists, engagement does not.

decision_process.narrative: A phased, dated process is documented: RFI issued 22 Jun 2026 with an original 6 Jul 2026 deadline, extended to 13 Jul 2026 (today) after Zycus requested more time; indicative ITT issue/close and evaluation/award windows run Aug-Sep 2026 per the CRM MEDDPICC record (7 Jul 2026); Zycus submitted its written RFI response on 10 Jul 2026, three days early, and asked the council to confirm timing for notifying vendors of next-stage progression. The one open sub-item is route to market: the current Next Step still flags closing out the RM6285 Lot 2 framework-route clarification.

decision_criteria.narrative: The RFI document (forwarded via Sophie Onyeks, 22 Jun 2026) explicitly scopes a cloud platform focused on upstream Source-to-Contract (strategic sourcing, CLM, supplier onboarding/management, intake, governance/workflow/audit), mandates integration with the relevant stakeholder (ERP) and the UK Government Central Digital Platform, and the CRM MEDDPICC record (7 Jul 2026) adds that full AP automation/invoicing is explicitly out of scope. Corroborated directly on the 22 Apr 2026 call by Sophie: 'it would need to integrate with Oracle... so Oracle is your major.'

north_star_verdict.math: Stage entered Formal Evaluation 2026-06-25 (12 days in stage as of 2026-07-07). Close date 2027-04-22 is 289 days out. Council's own indicative timeline: RFI due 6 Jul 2026 (passed, response still being finalized per the 6 Jul 2026 Next Step update), ITT issue 13 Jul 2026 (in ~6 days), ITT close 7 Aug 2026 (25-day window, shorter than the 4-6 weeks Zycus flagged as typical). north_star_verdict.headline: Birmingham is genuinely engaged on the RFI thread, but Zycus's two most consequential clarification questions (ITT timeframe and RM6285 Lot 2 framework access, sent 23 Jun 2026, chased 29 Jun and 1 Jul 2026) remain unanswered six days before the 13 Jul 2026 ITT issue date, stalling submission planning.

flags: Multi-vendor competitive process (7 Gartner MQ vendors) with no visibility into the other 6 rivals — differentiate aggressively in the RFI response on AI/Merlin control tower (the council is 'very interested in AI'), contract management depth (the core pain: manual Excel/SharePoint processes, no single view of risk), and Oracle ERP integration (mandatory requirement). The RFI response is the first impression and the gate to demo shortlist; a generic or weak response will eliminate Zycus before any champion-building opportunity.

No champion established — Sophie Onyeks (IT Delivery Manager, Influencer role) is engaged and helpful but explicitly 'not one of the big people doing the evaluations' and lacks decision authority. Greig Evans (Procurement Lead, Evaluator role) owns the RFI review and will evaluate vendors but has not yet engaged with Zycus. Lucy Moody (Assistant Director, Executive Sponsor) and John Coyne (Procurement Director, Economic Buyer/Decision Maker) have not appeared on calls. Deliver a standout RFI response by 6 July 2026 to secure a demo slot, then use the demo/workshop phase to build a champion among the evaluators (ideally Greig Evans or a senior procurement lead with access to John Coyne) and convert Lucy Moody's passive sponsorship into active advocacy. Without a champion, Zycus will lose in the evaluation scoring even with strong product fit.

Differentiate on AI and contract management depth in RFI response — buyer explicitly wants AI capabilities (new RFI question added) and has primary pain in manual contract management (Excel/SharePoint, no obligations tracking, no risk dashboards). Merlin agentic AI (intake control tower, autonomous negotiation, auto-contract-read) and full CLM platform (renewals, obligations, performance management, single supplier view) are the wedge vs competitors. Make AI use cases and CLM depth the centerpiece of the RFI response and any follow-on workshop.
Two blocking clarification questions (ITT timeframe, RM6285 Lot 2 eligibility) are unanswered days before the 13 Jul 2026 ITT issue; resolving them is the single highest-leverage lever now.

competitive_position.summary: Intent, the council's current procurement platform, is the only named competitor and is being actively displaced for lacking real contract-management capability (22 Apr 2026 call). deal_scores_evidence.summary: An early-stage but genuinely live RFP motion: the written response landed ahead of deadline and the buyer is still replying, but no economic buyer or executive sponsor has engaged directly and the competitive field remains largely unconfirmed.

deal_risk.text: No confirmed champion -- the CRM MEDDPICC record itself (7 Jul 2026) rates the council's RFI-coordinating contact's champion status as tentative and not yet established.
Economic buyer and executive sponsor unengaged -- a rival with a stronger executive relationship could win on trust before Zycus reaches John Coyne or Lucy Moody.

Route-to-market compliance risk -- Zycus's Crown Commercial Service framework listing was still pending as of the 22 Apr 2026 call ('we've just applied for Crown Commercial Services... it said June is when we'll be on there'), and RM6285 Lot 2 listing remains unresolved.

win_position.text: Live RFP response landed early -- Zycus submitted its written RFI reply on 10 Jul 2026, three days ahead of the (extended) 13 Jul 2026 deadline, leading with the connected S2C platform, Merlin/ANA, and Oracle integration story.
Deal is strengthening on the CRM record -- amount rose from $150,000 to $250,000 on 12 May 2026 and the stage advanced from Qualified to Formal Evaluation on 25 Jun 2026.

No economic buyer engaged yet -- Decision Maker John Coyne and titled Executive Sponsor Lucy Moody have had zero direct contact with Zycus since being named on the 22 Apr 2026 discovery call; all dialogue has run through Sophie Onyeks, who told Zycus on that call, 'I'm not gonna be one of the big people doing the evaluations... I'm literally just running things.'

Competing blind in an 8-way field -- per the CRM MEDDPICC record (updated 7 Jul 2026) the council is running an 8-way competitive RFI; beyond the incumbent Intent (contract expiring Aug 2027 per the current Next Step), the other rivals are unconfirmed.
Framework/route-to-market risk open -- Zycus is not a named supplier on the council's stated preferred RM6285 Lot 2 route, and the current Next Step still flags closing out that clarification.

deal_momentum.text: Genuine two-way buyer contact inside the last week -- Sophie Onyeks replied 'Correct' on 7 Jul 2026 confirming the RFI submission channel, and the RFI response itself went in on 10 Jul 2026 (3 days ago).
Concrete forward dates exist on both sides -- the council's own indicative RFI/ITT timetable and Zycus's Next Step carry three more dated milestones (21 Jul, 5 Aug, and through Aug 2026).

One rep-initiated ask is still awaiting reply -- Claire Hudson's 10 Jul 2026 submission email asked the council to confirm timing for the next-stage decision; no buyer reply has landed as of 13 Jul 2026 (3 days).
Only one recorded call in 82 days -- the single Avoma-covered touchpoint remains the 22 Apr 2026 discovery call; everything since has been email, so engagement depth beyond Sophie Onyeks is unproven.

customer_commitment.text: Buyer is actively working the RFI process rather than going quiet -- Sophie Onyeks pushed back for clarity on Zycus's own clarification question ('could you expand on what information you need on purchase volumes and why?', 25 Jun 2026).
No buyer-voiced preference for Zycus has surfaced -- evaluation of the 8-way field has not begun.
